name: Build
whenUsed: Combine directly in the service glass.
steps: Lift once from bottom to top.
practiceExercise: Practice building three highballs with identical fill lines.

name: Shake
whenUsed: Mix citrus, dairy, egg, juice, or other opaque ingredients with ice.
commonMistakes: Letting the drink sit on ice
practiceExercise: Shake water with ice and compare temperature at 6, 10, and 14 seconds.

name: Dry Shake
whenUsed: Shake an egg-white or foaming drink without ice before the cold shake.
whyItWorks: Builds foam before ice suppresses emulsification.
practiceExercise: Practice with aquafaba and water to observe foam formation.

name: Reverse Dry Shake
whenUsed: Shake with ice first, strain out ice, then shake again without ice.
whyItWorks: Creates a fine, polished foam cap.
commonMistakes: Leaving ice chips in second shake
practiceExercise: Compare dry-shake and reverse-dry-shake foam after five minutes.

name: Stir
whenUsed: Mix clear, spirit-forward drinks with ice in a mixing glass.
whyItWorks: Chills and dilutes while preserving clarity and silky texture.
practiceExercise: Stir water over ice and track dilution by weight.

name: Muddle
whenUsed: Press fruit, herbs, or sugar to release juice or aroma.
steps: Add the ingredient to a stable vessel.
Stop when juice or aroma is released.
Inspect for excessive pith or debris.
practiceExercise: Muddle lime lightly and aggressively, then taste the bitterness difference.

name: Roll
whenUsed: Pass a drink gently between two tins.
whyItWorks: Combines thick savory ingredients without excessive foam.
steps: Pour into second tin from a controlled height.
Pour or strain into service glass.
practiceExercise: Roll tomato juice and water, comparing texture to shaking.

name: Throw
whenUsed: Pour a drink in a long controlled stream between tins.
whyItWorks: Aerates and chills while creating a lighter texture than shaking.
steps: Repeat while keeping ice in one tin.
practiceExercise: Practice first with water over a sink.

name: Blend
whenUsed: Process a drink with measured ice in a blender.
practiceExercise: Blend identical recipes with different ice weights.

name: Layer
whenUsed: Place liquids in visible bands based on density.
steps: Float next layer over a spoon.
practiceExercise: Practice with colored sugar syrups of different concentrations.

name: Float
whenUsed: Place a measured ingredient on top of a drink.
whyItWorks: Concentrates aroma or creates a visual top layer.
practiceExercise: Float water dyed two colors over syrup.

name: Rim
whenUsed: Apply salt, sugar, spice, or another coating to part of a glass edge.
whyItWorks: Adds a controlled seasoning choice with each sip.
steps: Chill and dry the glass exterior.
Keep coating out of the drink.
commonMistakes: Rimming the full glass without asking
practiceExercise: Practice a clean half rim with no material inside the glass.

name: Express Citrus Oils
whenUsed: Release aromatic oils from a citrus peel over a finished drink.
whyItWorks: Adds volatile aroma at the point of service.
steps: Cut a broad peel with little pith.
Place or discard according to recipe.
practiceExercise: Express peels over water under a light to see the oil mist.

name: Fine Strain
whenUsed: Pass a drink through a fine mesh strainer.
whyItWorks: Removes small ice, herb, fruit, or egg particles.
practiceExercise: Shake mint with water and compare single vs fine strain.

name: Double Strain
whenUsed: Use a Hawthorne and fine strainer together.
practiceExercise: Practice pouring accurately through both strainers.

name: Swizzle
whenUsed: Rotate a swizzle stick rapidly through crushed ice.
whyItWorks: Chills and dilutes tropical and julep-style drinks in the glass.
steps: Add more ice as level falls.
practiceExercise: Swizzle water until the glass frosts and record the time.
